summary: A well‑rehearsed, in‑sync 1991 show with standout versions of Terrapin Station, Ramble on Rose, Help on the Way and He's Gone, captured in a great‑sounding recording.
review: Both reviewers rave about the tight, rehearsed playing of the band – noting Garcia, Lesh, Weir, Hart, Kreutzmann, Welnick and Hornsby – and highlight several superb song performances. The recording is praised as “great,” though no technical details are given, making it a highly enjoyable listen overall.

ai_rating.rationale: Both reviews are very positive (5 and 4 stars) and emphasize strong musical performance; recording quality is described as great, yielding a high combined rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Reviewer calls it a “great recording” with no reported flaws; no specifics on soundboard vs. audience.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The band is described as well‑rehearsed and in sync, cutting heads across all three nights, indicating a smooth, energetic flow throughout the set.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised as part of an in‑sync, well‑rehearsed band.
band_member_comments.Phil: Mentioned among the tight rhythm section contributing to the show's cohesion.
band_member_comments.Bob: Included in the list of players delivering a polished performance.
band_member_comments.Keys: Bruce Hornsby noted for his keyboard work adding depth to the set.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill Kreutzmann and Mickey Hart highlighted as part of the solid, synchronized rhythm foundation.
